title: Structured Reference to Retrieval Chunk Pipeline Pattern
summary: A pipeline pattern for converting structured reference records (JSON) into retrieval-ready chunks for full-text search indexing. Each reference record is split into sections (overview, steps, API, prompt_contract, verification, failure_modes, handoff), each section becomes a chunk with a stable chunk_id (record_id-section), and the chunk content includes header metadata (record_id, type, agent targets, goal, evidence level, source URL) prepended to the section body for search context. A rough token estimator provides lightweight size metadata. All chunks are written both per-record and as a combined manifest.
why_reusable: Any knowledge base or RAG system that ingests structured reference documents needs a chunking pipeline. The section-based splitting with metadata headers and token estimation is directly applicable to documentation sites, internal wikis, API reference docs, and curated knowledge bases.
adapter_target: Generic content chunking pipeline — parameterize the section definitions, metadata header format, and output paths.
implementation_notes: Each reference record is a JSON file with defined sections
Sections are defined as (section_key, title, body_parts) tuples — extensible per domain
Chunk content prepends metadata header (record_id, type, goal, evidence, source) for search context
Stable chunk_id format: {record_id}-{section} ensures predictable IDs across rebuilds
Rough token estimator: word count based on whitespace splitting — fast, not precise
Output: per-record chunk JSON + combined all_chunks manifest for index building
tests_required: Unit test: single record produces expected number of chunks
Unit test: chunk_id format is stable across rebuilds
Unit test: metadata header is included in chunk content
Unit test: empty sections are skipped (no empty chunks)
Integration test: all chunks from all records parse as valid JSON
risks: Section definitions are domain-specific and need adjustment for different content types
Token estimation is rough (word count) — not suitable for strict token budgets
Chunk content size is unbounded — very long sections produce very large chunks
Metadata header duplication across chunks adds search noise for common terms
do_not_do: Do not create chunks for empty sections — they waste index space
Do not rely on word-count tokens for LLM context budgeting — use a real tokenizer
Do not change chunk_id format after deploying the search system — it breaks references
notes: Extracted from DataBase scripts/agent_workflow/make_chunks.py (97 lines). The pattern generalizes to any structured-document-to-chunks pipeline.
